purpose: Define how a discovered transition becomes executable by proving dependency closure across shells, surfaces, receipts, and evidence.
core_rule: A transition may be discovered before it is executable, but it may not execute until dependency closure is proven.

shell_closure.meaning: All required authority shells have known canonical surfaces.

surface_closure.meaning: Every workflow/tool/data/capability surface required by the transition exists in the canonical map.

receipt_closure.meaning: All receipts required to prove the transition can be emitted or already exist.

evidence_closure.meaning: Observed receipts/reports support the claimed transition state without contradiction.

authority_closure.meaning: The action remains within the declared scope of mapped surfaces.

requires: transition not sufficiently discovered or no required surfaces identified
one or more closure axes open/blocked/contradicted
evidence proves transition cannot be admissible under current table
transition contradicts invariant or would require bypassing canonical surfaces

invariants: No transition can execute from discovery state alone.
No transition can close authority by creating a new unmapped authority surface.
A lower-shell dependency may be discovered by a higher transition, but the higher transition remains blocked until closure is proven.
A fresh hash, repeated upload, or renamed bundle is not dependency closure.
Workflow/tool mutation is not ordinary data evolution.
